title: Warm Comforting German Semolina Pudding
description: A comforting, diabetic-friendly German dessert featuring semolina, often served warm and topped with a sweet syrup. This classic recipe is easy to make and perfect for a chilly evening.

ingredients.name: milk
ingredients.notes: whole milk or a non-dairy milk alternative

instructions: Combine semolina flour, sugar, and salt in a medium saucepan.
Gradually add milk, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and comes to a boil.
Remove from heat and stir in butter, eggs, and vanilla extract.
Stir in brine water and let cool slightly.
Pour into individual serving cups and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or until chilled.
If desired, dust with confectioners' sugar before serving.
Heat pudding in the microwave or in a saucepan over low heat until warm.
Serve immediately, garnished with sugar or fresh fruit if desired.
